# terrible_cisco_mso_ndo_route_map_policy_route_control_context (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `action` (String) The action of the Route Control context. Defaults to `permit` when unset during creation.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) The description of the Route Control context. Providing an empty string `description=""` will remove description from the Route Control context.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `match_rules` (String) The list of Match Rule Policy references. Providing an empty list `match_rules=[]` will remove Match Rule Policies from the Route Control context. When the `match_rules` is null the update will not change existing configuration. The old `match_rules` list will be replaced by the new list during an update.
- `name` (String) The name of the Route Control context.
- `order` (Number) The order of the Route Control context. The value must be a number between 0 and 9. Defaults to `0` when unset during creation.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `route_map_policy` (String) The name of the Route Map Policy for Route Control. This parameter or `route_map_policy_uuid` is required.
- `route_map_policy_uuid` (String) The UUID of the Route Map Policy for Route Control. This parameter or `route_map_policy` is required.
- `set_rule` (String) The Set Rule Policy reference details for the Route Control context. Providing an empty dictionary `set_rule={}` will remove Set Rule Policy from the Route Control context.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Use `absent` for removing. Use `query` for listing an object or multiple objects. Use `present` for creating or updating.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `template` (String) The name of the template. The template must be a tenant template. This parameter or `template_id` is required.
- `template_id` (String) The ID of the tenant template. This parameter or `template` is required.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
